title: Fuel and emission control structure description - Overview

blocks.text: Fuel and emission control structure description - Overview

The engine installed in this vehicle complies with the emission regulations in the table and has been certified for each model by adopting the emission control system of "engine modification + exhaust gas recirculation system + three-way catalyst system". The blow-by gas reduction system is a closed type. The fuel evaporation gas emission control device uses a canister system. On-board failure detection system for carbon monoxide and other emission prevention devices is installed.

Two intake valves and two exhaust valves are placed in each pent roof type combustion chamber. In addition, engine parameters such as compression ratio, ignition timing, and valve opening/closing timing are optimized to obtain high intake/exhaust efficiency and to improve combustion efficiency. The PGM-FI ECU uses an electronic fuel injection system with a LAF (Linear Air Fuel Ratio) sensor and a secondary O 2 The air-fuel ratio is controlled by correcting the fuel injection amount based on the feedback signal from the sensor.

The exhaust gas recirculation system is electronically controlled. This device is designed to reduce NOx levels by returning exhaust gas in a controlled amount appropriate to the operating conditions.
The three-way catalytic converter is a platinum-palladium-rhodium monolithic catalytic converter mounted directly under the cylinder head. The catalytic converter oxidizes CO-HC and reduces NOx.
